(born September 16, 1983) is a former American [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") who fought in the [Featherweight](/wiki/Featherweight_(MMA) "Featherweight (MMA)") division. He has competed for the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [WEC](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting"), and was a contestant on [The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_GSP_vs._Team_Koscheck "The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck").

**Pablo Garza** (born September 16, 1983) is a former American [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") who fought in the [Featherweight](/wiki/Featherweight_(MMA) "Featherweight (MMA)") division. He has competed for the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [WEC](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting"), and was a contestant on [The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_GSP_vs._Team_Koscheck "The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pablo_Garza_(fighter)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Garza was born in [Wasco, California](/wiki/Wasco,_California "Wasco, California"), on September 16th, 1983, to parents who both emigrated from [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"). Garza and his family moved to North Dakota, where he attended high school and played baseball, basketball, and track. He continued to play basketball in college for two years at the [University of Jamestown](/wiki/University_of_Jamestown "University of Jamestown"), as a [point guard](/wiki/Point_guard "Point guard"). He eventually earned a degree in exercise science and personal training at the [University of North Dakota](/wiki/University_of_North_Dakota "University of North Dakota"). While Garza was in college, he worked two jobs at a factory making windmill blades, and also did an internship at a gym.

## Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pablo_Garza_(fighter)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

Garza trains out of Dylan Spicers' MMA gym the Academy of Combat Arts in [Fargo, North Dakota](/wiki/Fargo,_North_Dakota "Fargo, North Dakota"), as well as the Academy in [Brooklyn Center, Minnesota](/wiki/Brooklyn_Center,_Minnesota "Brooklyn Center, Minnesota") with the likes of [Jacob Volkmann](/wiki/Jacob_Volkmann "Jacob Volkmann"), [Nik Lentz](/wiki/Nik_Lentz "Nik Lentz"), [Pat Barry](/wiki/Pat_Barry_(kickboxer) "Pat Barry (kickboxer)") and coach Greg Nelson.[[3]](#cite_note-sherdog-3)
Garza is a predominantly ground-based muay thai fighter,[[4]](#cite_note-4) where he has managed to secure several [submission](/wiki/Submission "Submission") victories.[[3]](#cite_note-sherdog-3) A [Fargo, North Dakota](/wiki/Fargo,_North_Dakota "Fargo, North Dakota")-based Mexican American from [Grafton, North Dakota](/wiki/Grafton,_North_Dakota "Grafton, North Dakota"),[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Prior to joining the UFC, Garza appeared for promotions such as [King of the Cage](/wiki/King_of_the_Cage "King of the Cage") and Brutal Fight Night.[[3]](#cite_note-sherdog-3)

### The Ultimate Fighter

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pablo_Garza_(fighter)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The Ultimate Fighter")]

Garza then signed with the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") to appear on *[The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_GSP_vs._Team_Koscheck "The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck")*.[[6]](#cite_note-tuf12-6)

In the debut episode, Garza lost to touted prospect[[7]](#cite_note-wec51-7) [Michael Johnson](/wiki/Michael_Johnson_(fighter) "Michael Johnson (fighter)") in a two-round decision.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### Outside promotions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pablo_Garza_(fighter)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Outside promotions")]

After leaving the UFC, Garza fought in smaller, outside promotions. Before his *Ultimate Fighter* fight with Michael Johnson had even aired on television, Garza faced William Joplin, winning via unanimous decision.[[3]](#cite_note-sherdog-3) The fight, which was predominantly stand-up based, saw Garza break the nose of Joplin, before taking control of the fight.[[9]](#cite_note-hpr1-9)

Garza then faced Aaron Steele on September 11, 2010. In preparation for the fight, Garza visited [Minnesota Martial Arts Academy](/wiki/Minnesota_Martial_Arts_Academy "Minnesota Martial Arts Academy"), training with [Sean Sherk](/wiki/Sean_Sherk "Sean Sherk"), [Jacob Volkmann](/wiki/Jacob_Volkmann "Jacob Volkmann") and Greg Nelson.[[9]](#cite_note-hpr1-9) Garza went on to win the fight via TKO (punches) late in the third round.[[3]](#cite_note-sherdog-3)

### World Extreme Cagefighting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pablo_Garza_(fighter)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: World Extreme Cagefighting")]

Garza was then signed by the UFC's sister promotion, World Extreme Cagefighting. Replacing [Jason Reinhardt](/wiki/Jason_Reinhardt "Jason Reinhardt"), Garza stepped up to face [Tiequan Zhang](/wiki/Tiequan_Zhang "Tiequan Zhang") on the undercard of [WEC 51](/wiki/WEC_51 "WEC 51") on 5 days notice.[[7]](#cite_note-wec51-7) He lost the fight via [submission](/wiki/Submission "Submission") in the first round.

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pablo_Garza_(fighter)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

In October 2010, World Extreme Cagefighting merged with the Ultimate Fighting Championship. As part of the merger, all WEC fighters were transferred to the UFC.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Garza made his UFC and featherweight debut against [Fredson Paixão](/wiki/Fredson_Paix%C3%A3o "Fredson Paixão") at [The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_GSP_vs._Team_Koscheck_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck Finale").[[11]](#cite_note-11) This was the first ever featherweight fight in the UFC. Garza knocked Paixão out 51 seconds into the first round with a flying knee. After being unconscious for nearly two minutes, Paixão was on his back for close to four minutes while being given oxygen, before being placed in a [neck brace](/wiki/Neck_brace "Neck brace") and carried out on a stretcher. Garza earned *Knockout of the Night* honors for the performance.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

Garza defeated [Yves Jabouin](/wiki/Yves_Jabouin "Yves Jabouin") on April 30, 2011 at [UFC 129](/wiki/UFC_129 "UFC 129") via first round submission due to a flying triangle choke.[[13]](#cite_note-13) The finish earned him *Submission of the Night* honors and $129,000.

Garza faced [Dustin Poirier](/wiki/Dustin_Poirier "Dustin Poirier") on November 12, 2011 at [UFC on Fox 1](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_1 "UFC on Fox 1").[[14]](#cite_note-14) He lost the fight in the second round after tapping out to a d'arce choke.

Garza next faced [Dennis Bermudez](/wiki/Dennis_Bermudez "Dennis Bermudez") at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3") on May 5, 2012.[[15]](#cite_note-15) He lost the fight via unanimous decision (30-27, 30-27, 30-27).

Garza was expected to face [Josh Grispi](/wiki/Josh_Grispi "Josh Grispi") on August 4, 2012 at [UFC on FOX 4](/wiki/UFC_on_FOX_4 "UFC on FOX 4").[[16]](#cite_note-16) However, Garza was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Rani Yahya](/wiki/Rani_Yahya "Rani Yahya").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Garza faced [Mark Hominick](/wiki/Mark_Hominick "Mark Hominick") on November 17, 2012 at [UFC 154](/wiki/UFC_154 "UFC 154").[[18]](#cite_note-18) He won the fight by unanimous decision.

Garza faced [Diego Brandão](/wiki/Diego_Brand%C3%A3o "Diego Brandão") on April 6, 2013 at [UFC on Fuel TV 9](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV_9 "UFC on Fuel TV 9").[[19]](#cite_note-19) He lost the fight via first round submission and was subsequently released from the promotion.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### Post UFC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pablo_Garza_(fighter)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Post UFC")]

Shortly after his release from the UFC, Garza relocated to [Oslo](/wiki/Oslo "Oslo"), [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). It was expected that he would receive offers from the elite European MMA promotions such as Britain's [Cage Warriors](/wiki/Cage_Warriors "Cage Warriors") and [BAMMA](/wiki/BAMMA "BAMMA") or Sweden's Superior Challenge. However, Garza has not competed since his release from the UFC.

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pablo_Garza_(fighter)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + **UFC.com Awards**
    - 2010: [Ranked #5 Knockout of the Year](/wiki/2010_in_UFC#2010_UFC.com_awards "2010 in UFC") vs. [Fredson Paixão](/wiki/Fredson_Paix%C3%A3o "Fredson Paixão")[[21]](#cite_note-21)
    - 2011: [Ranked #3 Submission of the Year](/wiki/2011_in_UFC#2011_UFC.com_awards "2011 in UFC") vs. [Yves Jabouin](/wiki/Yves_Jabouin "Yves Jabouin")[[22]](#cite_note-22)
